old_claim.note: Iron Working provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No ancient iron-working edge remains because weapon material is remote infrastructure, not the defining tactical dependency of the scoped formation.

source_shape.source_locator: Object description defines the tactical relationship through ranked pike protection, musketeer flanks, volley fire, and reloading shelter.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The museum explains pike-and-shot as coordinated infantry deployment rather than as a metal-production innovation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A source about prehistoric meteoritic beads could not support this early modern tactical edge, and the direct museum account does not require it.

ontology_before: The tactical formation depended directly on a prehistoric material-processing node through an unrelated iron-bead citation.
ontology_after: The formation depends on the musketry tactic it combines with pike protection; weapon metallurgy remains upstream context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: pike_and_shot_formations no longer directly depends on iron_working.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to manufacturing iron pikes or firearm components rather than deploying infantry.
short_reason: Ancient iron production is not the direct cause of this tactic.
